Why I Moved from Angular to React (And When I Went Back)
After 2 years of React-only development, I had to revisit Angular for a project. Here's what I learned about both ecosystems and when each shines.

I've been working with both Angular and React for over 3 years now, building applications for US clients ranging from startups to enterprise. The framework debate is real, and I've changed my mind several times.

## My React Journey

React won me over with its simplicity and flexibility. The component model is intuitive, the ecosystem is massive, and it gets out of your way.

## Why I Went Back to Angular

A fintech project required strict typing, dependency injection, and a batteries-included framework. Angular's opinionated nature became a feature, not a bug.

## The Verdict

Use React when you want flexibility and a large ecosystem. Use Angular when you need structure and enterprise features out of the box. Both are excellent.

E-commerce Platform Migration: From Monolith to Microservices
Led the migration of a legacy e-commerce monolith to a microservices architecture, handling 50K+ daily transactions.
A US-based retail client was experiencing 4+ second load times and frequent outages during peak sales, losing an estimated $15K/hour in revenue.

Architected and led the migration from a monolithic Ruby on Rails application to a microservices-based system using Node.js, React storefront, and Shopify Plus for checkout. Implemented event-driven communication with message queues and Redis caching.
Reduced page load times by 73%, eliminated downtime during peak traffic, and enabled the client to scale their Black Friday sales 3x without any infrastructure changes.
Lead Engineer - Designed architecture, led 3-developer team, and implemented core migration.

This project represents one of the most impactful migrations I've led. The client, a mid-size US retailer, was bleeding revenue due to their legacy system's inability to handle growing traffic.

## The Challenge

Their existing monolithic Rails application was a 7-year-old codebase that had grown organically. Every deployment was risky, and the database was a single point of failure.

## The Approach

1. **Strangler Fig Pattern**: Gradually extracted services from the monolith
2. **Shopify Plus Integration**: Moved checkout to a proven, scalable platform
3. **React Storefront**: Built a fast, modern storefront with React
4. **Event-Driven Architecture**: Implemented async communication between services

## Results

The migration was completed in 4 months with zero downtime. The client saw immediate improvements in performance and reliability.

Real-Time Analytics Dashboard for FinTech Startup
Built a real-time analytics dashboard processing 2M+ events/day, enabling data-driven decisions for a Series A startup.
A US fintech startup had no visibility into their transaction data. Manual reporting took 3 days per week, and executives couldn't make real-time decisions about fraud detection and user behavior.

Designed and built a real-time analytics pipeline using Python for data ingestion, MongoDB for flexible storage, and a React/Angular dashboard with live WebSocket updates. Implemented custom aggregation pipelines that processed 2M+ daily events.

Reduced reporting time from 3 days to real-time, caught $50K+ in fraudulent transactions in the first month, and provided the executive team with instant KPI visibility that supported their Series B fundraising.
Full-Stack Engineer - Built entire pipeline from data ingestion to dashboard UI.

## The Challenge

This Series A fintech startup was flying blind. Their transaction volume was growing 30% month-over-month, but their reporting capabilities couldn't keep up.

## The Solution

I built a complete analytics pipeline:

1. **Data Ingestion Layer**: Python microservice processing events in real-time
2. **Storage**: MongoDB with optimized aggregation pipelines
3. **Dashboard**: Angular-based real-time visualization with WebSocket updates
4. **Alerts**: Automated fraud detection alerts

## The Impact

The real-time dashboard became the central tool for the executive team. They used the data to demonstrate growth metrics during their Series B pitch, successfully raising $8M.

Rebuilt a healthcare scheduling system that reduced staff admin time by 40% and eliminated double-booking for a 200+ provider network.

A US healthcare network with 200+ providers was managing appointments through a legacy Java desktop application. Double-bookings were common, staff spent 15+ hours/week on scheduling conflicts, and patients couldn't book online.

Built a modern web-based scheduling platform using Java Spring Boot backend, React frontend with drag-and-drop calendar, and PostgreSQL for complex availability rules. Implemented smart conflict detection, patient self-service booking, and automated reminders.
Eliminated 95% of scheduling conflicts, reduced administrative time by 40%, enabled patient self-service booking (60% adoption in first quarter), and improved patient satisfaction scores by 25%.
Backend Lead & Full-Stack Developer - Designed the scheduling engine and led frontend implementation.

## The Challenge

This healthcare network was growing fast, but their scheduling system was stuck in the past. The legacy Java desktop app couldn't handle the complexity of 200+ providers with different schedules, specialties, and insurance constraints.

## The Solution

1. **Smart Scheduling Engine**: Java Spring Boot backend with complex business rules
2. **Interactive Calendar**: React-based drag-and-drop interface for staff
3. **Patient Portal**: Self-service booking with real-time availability
4. **Notification System**: Automated SMS and email reminders

## The Results

The platform transformed how the network manages appointments. Staff could focus on patient care instead of phone scheduling, and patients loved the convenience of online booking.

Shopify App: Automated Inventory Sync for Multi-Channel Sellers
Developed a Shopify app that synchronizes inventory across 5+ sales channels in real-time, saving merchants 20+ hours/week.
Multi-channel sellers were manually updating inventory across Shopify, Amazon, eBay, and their own website. Stock discrepancies led to overselling, cancelled orders, and negative reviews.

Built a Shopify app using Ruby on Rails that listens to inventory events via Shopify webhooks, syncs to connected platforms through their APIs, and provides a dashboard for monitoring stock levels. Implemented conflict resolution algorithms and real-time alerts.
Eliminated 99% of overselling incidents, saved merchants an average of 20 hours/week on manual inventory management, and achieved 500+ active installs within 6 months of launch.
Solo Developer - Conceived, built, and launched the app from scratch.

## The Challenge

Multi-channel e-commerce is a inventory nightmare. When you sell on Shopify, Amazon, eBay, Walmart, and your own website simultaneously, keeping stock levels synchronized is nearly impossible manually.

## The Solution

I built a Shopify app that:

1. **Real-Time Sync**: Listens to Shopify inventory webhooks and pushes updates to all connected channels
2. **Conflict Resolution**: Smart algorithms handle simultaneous updates across platforms
3. **Dashboard**: Clean React UI showing stock levels across all channels
4. **Alerts**: Proactive notifications when stock is critically low

## The Results

The app quickly gained traction in the Shopify App Store. Merchants reported dramatic time savings and virtually eliminated overselling issues.
